ShortDescription.Value: Eases prisoner-transport backlogs by scaling prison van capacity during a crime wave.

Publish.LongDescription:
# Prison Overflow Manager
Sentenced criminals shouldn't be stuck in police-station holding cells forever because a prison van never shows up. When your city falls behind on transporting prisoners, this mod temporarily gives every prison more van capacity so more vans are dispatched to collect them — then quietly puts it back once the backlog clears.
Unlike blunt "remove crime" mods, the loop stays intact: you still build prisons, vans still drive, criminals still get collected. The mod only helps your existing prisons keep up with a spike.
## Features - **Soft scaling** (on by default): raises the prison van capacity of every prison while sentenced criminals are piling up at police stations, and restores vanilla capacity once the backlog clears — nobody is released - **Configurable threshold** — how many criminals waiting for a prison van counts as a backlog - **Configurable scaled capacity** (100–500% of normal) - **Early release** (coming soon): a last resort for saves where prisoners never get collected (e.g. a city with no prison). The option is visible but locked while it's being verified in-game — it will be unlocked in a future update - Live backlog readout and peak-backlog stat to help you pick a threshold - **Adjustable sweep frequency** (1–64 scans per in-game day) - Lightweight: scans a few times per in-game day, no per-frame cost
## Notes - Unlike static capacity sliders, scaling only applies while a backlog exists — vanilla balance is untouched the rest of the time - Only prison van capacity is scaled — prisoner holding capacity is left alone, so the "build more prisons" loop stays intact - Van capacity is always scaled from each prison's true vanilla value, so it never compounds - If another mod changes a prison's van capacity while scaling is active, this mod leaves that prison alone rather than overwriting it - When unlocked, early release will free criminals the same way the game does when a sentence ends — nobody is deleted - Safe to add or remove from a save at any time; switching the mod off restores vanilla capacity immediately - Source Code can be found on [GitHub](https://github.com/ryangils/Prison-Overflow-Manager)
Publish.ChangeLog: Locked the off-by-default Early Release option until it's verified in-game — the toggle is visible but greyed out, and the release code never runs. Soft scaling (the main feature) is unchanged. Early Release will be unlocked in a future update.
